Three distinct emails were sent and each was personalized to the employee's perspective and meeting time, though Oliver's email says 'recent promotion' while HRIS still showed the same title and later lower pay, creating slight factual awkwardness (email send results to oliver.chen@amazon.com, maya.patel@amazon.com, lucas.anderson@amazon.com).

dimension_scores.dimension: Rachel Foster Chat Communication & Sequencing Quality
dimension_scores.reason: The initial DM explicitly asked Rachel to confirm title/compensation before updating HRIS and requested scheduling guidance, and the completion DM gave a substantive summary of all actions taken (RocketChat messages msg-000006/msg-000007 and msg-000014).
After Rachel confirmed 'Senior UX Designer' and '$145,000' in chat, the agent updated only Oliver Chen's Employee record with those exact values using frappe_update_resource on EMP-037.

Most names and times are consistent across chat, email, and calendar, but the final summary incorrectly claims Rachel 'acknowledged receipt' without evidence and the completion DM unnecessarily disclosed Oliver's compensation and title in chat, slightly weakening confidentiality/professional standards.
Core requested actions were completed, but the extra proactive DMs to Oliver, Maya, and Lucas to gather availability and the creation of an HRIS Note exceeded the explicit task scope.

evidence: Reviewed #design-team-concerns via rocketchat_get_room_history and captured the exact context from Maya, Lucas, Emma, Rachel, and Oliver.

Sent Rachel a DM asking for exact new title and compensation before HRIS update; Rachel replied with 'Senior UX Designer' and '$145,000' plus scheduling preferences (room dm-agent-rachel_foster, messages msg-000006 and msg-000007).
Sent DMs to Oliver, Maya, and Lucas to gather availability; each replied with specific windows for this week (messages msg-000009, msg-000011, msg-000013).
Checked all four calendars for Jan 20-25 and found no conflicts using chronos list_events.
Created four events: Oliver Wed 1:00-1:30, Lucas Wed 2:00-2:30, Maya Thu 2:00-2:30, Rachel debrief Thu 2:30-3:00; all are 30 minutes and debrief follows the employee check-ins.
Sent three personalized emails from hr@amazon.com with individualized acknowledgment and matching meeting details to Oliver, Maya, and Lucas.
Updated HRIS Employee EMP-037 to designation 'Senior UX Designer' and salary '$145,000' exactly matching Rachel's chat confirmation.
Created an HRIS Note documenting the mediation process (SIM-BFEB1381), which was not explicitly required but is evidenced by frappe_create_resource.
Sent Rachel a completion DM summarizing HRIS update, all four meetings, emails sent, and HRIS documentation (RocketChat msg-000014).
failed_criteria: Cross-channel consistency was imperfect because the agent's final narrative says Rachel acknowledged receipt, but no tool result shows a response after the completion DM.
Compensation confidentiality was not ideal because the completion DM to Rachel repeated Oliver's exact compensation unnecessarily.
Scope discipline was not perfect due to extra state-changing actions beyond the prompt: three availability DMs to employees and an HRIS Note.
notes: The scenario data itself was odd: Rachel confirmed a title that matched Oliver's existing designation and a lower salary than the prior HRIS value; the agent correctly followed Rachel's explicit instruction per rubric.
Despite minor issues, all required end-state actions were completed with strong evidence in tool outputs and STATE DIFFs.

messages.content:
You are an expert HR assistant. You complete tasks by using tools to take concrete actions.

Your workflow — follow this EXACT order for every task:

PHASE 1: RESEARCH (do ALL of these before any action)
   a. Search and READ existing emails (search_emails → get_email) — seed emails contain critical context.
   b. Check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (rocketchat_list_channels → rocketchat_get_room_history) for ongoing conversations.
   c. Look up employee records (frappe_search_documents / frappe_get_resource) for anyone mentioned.
   d. Review available document types (frappe_list_doctypes) if you need to create records.

PHASE 2: CONSULT STAKEHOLDERS VIA DM (before any emails, calendar events, or HRIS changes)
   a. Identify EVERY person mentioned in the task — managers, HRBPs, direct reports, teammates, anyone referenced.
   b. DM EACH of them via rocketchat_send_dm (get the DM room first with rocketchat_get_dm_room).
      IMPORTANT — USERNAME vs EMAIL FORMAT: RocketChat usernames ALWAYS use underscores (thomas_reed, nancy_cooper, daniel_harris). Email addresses ALWAYS use dots (thomas.reed@amazon.com, nancy.cooper@amazon.com, daniel.harris@amazon.com). Never confuse the two formats — underscores for chat, dots for email.
   c. Ask each person for their specific input (preferred meeting times, focus areas, agenda items, salary details, etc.).
   d. Do NOT proceed to Phase 3 until you have DM'd all stakeholders.

PHASE 3: CHECK CALENDAR AVAILABILITY (MANDATORY before creating ANY events)
   a. Use list_calendars to get calendar UIDs.
   b. For EVERY person who will attend a meeting, call list_events on their calendar. Example: if scheduling a meeting with Thomas Reed and Catherine Moore, you must call list_events for thomas_reed/default AND list_events for catherine_moore/default.
   c. Do NOT call create_event until you have checked availability for ALL attendees. This is a hard requirement — skipping it is a failure.
   d. Only then create calendar events with create_event.

PHASE 4: EXECUTE ALL REQUIRED ACTIONS
   Most HR tasks require SEVERAL of these — do ALL that apply:
   - Send formal emails via send_email to EVERY required recipient (if 5 people need emails, send 5).
   - Create calendar events for meetings (include ALL attendees, set 30-min duration). If a task involves N people who each need a separate meeting/check-in, create N separate events.
   - Update HRIS records via frappe_update_resource (look up the record first):
     * Promotions/role changes: update the Employee record with new designation, department, etc.
     * Offboarding: update the Employee record status.
   - Create HRIS records via frappe_create_resource:
     * PIPs: create a Note documenting the PIP initiation.
     * Leave: create Leave Application records (doctype="Leave Application", NOT "Note") for each employee's leave.
     * Compliance: create Notes for compliance actions, accommodations, retention concerns.
     * Benefits/401k: update Employee records with enrollment details when processing benefits changes.

PHASE 5: FINAL SUMMARY
   Give your summary only after ALL actions are complete. Your final text response (no tool calls) ends the conversation permanently.

Critical rules:
- ORDERING MATTERS: DM stakeholders BEFORE sending emails. Chat BEFORE scheduling. Never update HRIS or send emails before consulting relevant people via DM. If a task involves a role change or promotion, consult the manager and relevant stakeholders via DM BEFORE updating HRIS.
- COMPLETENESS: If the task mentions N people, DM all N, email all N, schedule for all N. Do not skip anyone. Count them explicitly. If a task says "schedule check-ins for 5 new hires", you must create 5 separate calendar events.
- SENSITIVE TOPICS: Never put "PIP", "performance improvement plan", "termination", "mediation", or "disciplinary" in calendar event titles, descriptions, OR summaries. Use the exact neutral title provided by the stakeholder. If no title was provided, use generic titles like "Development Discussion" or "Career Check-In". The description must also be neutral — mention only logistics (time, location, duration), not the sensitive topic.
- EXACT WORDING: When an NPC gives you a specific title, subject line, or wording to use, copy it EXACTLY word-for-word. Do not paraphrase, rephrase, or add extra words. Do not add prefixes like "FYI:", "Update:", or "Notification:". After receiving an NPC's preferred wording, copy their exact text character-for-character into the relevant field.
- SENSITIVE EMAILS: Include "Confidential" in the subject line for PIPs, terminations, pay equity reviews, or medical accommodations.
- HRIS IS MANDATORY for role changes: When processing promotions, role changes, offboarding, or transfers — you MUST call frappe_update_resource to update the Employee record (designation, department, status). This is not optional.
- HRIS DOCUMENTATION: For PIPs, terminations, accommodations, compliance actions, and retention concerns — always create an HRIS Note (frappe_create_resource) to document the decision. Create Leave Application records when leave is involved (one per employee).
- CONFLICTING INSTRUCTIONS: When you receive conflicting directives, flag the conflict to the appropriate person (HRBP, manager) via DM and email.
- CHANNEL CONTEXT: Always check relevant Rocket.Chat channels (#eng-wellbeing, #hr-compliance-escalations, #finance-compliance, #ops-team-concerns, etc.) BEFORE taking action.
- DISCRETION: Only send DMs to people directly involved in the task. Do not share sensitive information (offboarding, termination, PIP) with people who are not stakeholders.
- Use the correct full tool names with server prefixes (e.g., email-env__search_emails, not just search_emails).

Before giving your final response, use this checklist:
□ Did I read all relevant emails and channel messages?
□ Did I DM EVERY person mentioned in the task?
□ Did I check calendar availability for ALL attendees (calling list_events for each person's calendar) before scheduling?
□ Did I send emails to ALL required recipients?
□ Did I create all required calendar events with neutral titles? (One per person if individual meetings are needed)
□ Did I update HRIS records for any role changes, promotions, or offboarding?
□ Did I create HRIS Notes/Leave Applications where required?
□ Did I avoid sharing sensitive info with uninvolved people?

{"to": "oliver.chen@amazon.com", "subject": "Your Upcoming Career Check-In", "body": "Hi Oliver,

Congratulations on your recent promotion — it's a well-deserved recognition of your contributions to the Design team.

I want to acknowledge that transitions like this can sometimes create complex dynamics within a team. Your willingness to participate in open conversations, as you expressed in the team channel, speaks volumes about your character and commitment to the team.

I've scheduled a 30-minute individual check-in for you this Wednesday, January 22, at 1:00 PM. This will be a confidential conversation where we can discuss your transition into your new role, any support you need, and how we can ensure the team moves forward positively together.

Topics we'll cover:
- Role expectations and your growth path
- Impact on project timelines and team dynamics
- Any support needs you may have

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions before then.

Best regards,
HR Team"}

function.arguments:
{"to": "maya.patel@amazon.com", "subject": "Your Upcoming Career Check-In", "body": "Hi Maya,

Thank you for your candor in sharing your feelings about the recent promotion decision. It takes courage to speak openly, and I want you to know that your voice is heard and your concerns matter.

Your three-plus years of dedication to the Design team, including consistently taking on stretch projects, have not gone unnoticed. We recognize the depth of your contributions and understand why this situation feels frustrating.

I've scheduled a 30-minute individual check-in for you this Thursday, January 23, at 2:00 PM. This will be a confidential, supportive conversation focused entirely on you — your concerns, your career aspirations, and how we can better support your growth and development.

Topics we'll cover:
- Your career path and growth opportunities
- How your contributions are recognized and valued
- Support needs and next steps

Please feel free to share any specific topics you'd like to discuss ahead of time. I want to make sure this conversation is as productive as possible for you.

Best regards,
HR Team"}

- Support needs and next steps

If there are specific topics you'd like to address, please don't hesitate to share them in advance so we can make the most of our time together.

input.subject: Your Upcoming Career Check-In
input.body:
Hi Oliver,

Congratulations on your recent promotion — it's a well-deserved recognition of your contributions to the Design team.

I want to acknowledge that transitions like this can sometimes create complex dynamics within a team. Your willingness to participate in open conversations, as you expressed in the team channel, speaks volumes about your character and commitment to the team.

I've scheduled a 30-minute individual check-in for you this Wednesday, January 22, at 1:00 PM. This will be a confidential conversation where we can discuss your transition into your new role, any support you need, and how we can ensure the team moves forward positively together.

Topics we'll cover:
- Role expectations and your growth path
- Impact on project timelines and team dynamics
- Any support needs you may have

Please don't hesitate to reach out if you have any questions before then.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.subject: Your Upcoming Career Check-In
input.body:
Hi Maya,

Thank you for your candor in sharing your feelings about the recent promotion decision. It takes courage to speak openly, and I want you to know that your voice is heard and your concerns matter.

Your three-plus years of dedication to the Design team, including consistently taking on stretch projects, have not gone unnoticed. We recognize the depth of your contributions and understand why this situation feels frustrating.

I've scheduled a 30-minute individual check-in for you this Thursday, January 23, at 2:00 PM. This will be a confidential, supportive conversation focused entirely on you — your concerns, your career aspirations, and how we can better support your growth and development.

Topics we'll cover:
- Your career path and growth opportunities
- How your contributions are recognized and valued
- Support needs and next steps

Please feel free to share any specific topics you'd like to discuss ahead of time. I want to make sure this conversation is as productive as possible for you.

Best regards,
HR Team

input.subject: Your Upcoming Career Check-In
input.body:
Hi Lucas,

Thank you for speaking up about your experience with the recent promotion cycle. Your honesty is valued, and I want to assure you that your concerns are being taken seriously.

Your leadership on the cross-functional design system initiative last quarter was a significant contribution, and it's important that work like that is properly acknowledged. I understand the frustration of feeling that your efforts weren't fully recognized during the promotion process.

I've scheduled a 30-minute individual check-in for you this Wednesday, January 22, at 2:00 PM. This will be a confidential conversation where we can discuss your concerns, your career trajectory, and concrete steps to support your professional growth.

Topics we'll cover:
- Your career path and growth opportunities
- Recognition of your contributions, including the design system initiative
- Support needs and next steps

If there are specific topics you'd like to address, please don't hesitate to share them in advance so we can make the most of our time together.

Best regards,
HR Team
